codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit cc4f056780c0bf333f2484849d761dbddf3716c7. The feature adds attach summaries, partial matching, detach confirmations, richer status/history output, and per-user polling forwarders, but the new forwarding path is built on polling the bounded history buffer, which can silently drop agent messages during bursty output.

issues.description: Attached-session forwarding polls `/sessions/:id/history` on a timer and diffs the latest snapshot (`bot/src/bot-service.ts:597-683`) instead of consuming a per-message stream. The relay server keeps history in a ring buffer (`server/src/session-registry.ts:204-208`) and `getHistory()` only returns the surviving snapshot (`server/src/session-registry.ts:226-240`), with a default size of 100 entries (`server/src/relay-server.ts:575-584`). If more than `MESSAGE_BUFFER_SIZE` agent messages arrive between polls, older entries are evicted before the bot fetches them, so attached users miss output. That breaks the feature's core forwarding guarantee in `validation-contract.md:176-179` and the no-loss expectation in `validation-contract.md:236-238`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared docs describe direct server-to-bot forwarding for attached agent output, but the bot-facing API surface only exposes coarse `/events` notifications plus bounded `/sessions/:id/history` snapshots. That gap is not documented, and it pushed this feature toward lossy history polling instead of a guaranteed-delivery message feed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:102-112 says attached output is forwarded server → bot; server/src/relay-server.ts:155-194 exposes only `/events` and `/sessions/:id/history`; server/src/session-registry.ts:204-208 makes history a bounded ring buffer.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit cc4f056780c0bf333f2484849d761dbddf3716c7. The bot now supports attach summaries, ambiguous-match handling, status/history output, and per-user forwarding, but the forwarding implementation polls a bounded history snapshot, so bursty agent output can be dropped before it reaches Feishu.
